summary: Backend + frontend e2e validation of (A) admin backup/restore center and (B) topbar sync indicator. Backend pytest: 16/16 passing in /app/backend/tests/test_backup.py — covers settings GET/PATCH (incl. HH:MM regex 422 reject), POST create (returns metadata, no .data, type='manual'), GET list (excludes .data, max_auto=365), GET {id} (downloadable doc with Content-Disposition: attachment header + per-collection .data dict), restore confirm-guard (400 without confirm), restore on invalid id (400 with Arabic detail), restore happy-path (200 + safety_backup name starting 'before_restore_'), import full snapshot, import malformed payload (400), audit log shape (op in backup/restore/delete/import + status + created_at + user_name), delete-last guard (400 Arabic), delete with 2+ (200). Teacher gets 403, unauthenticated gets 401/403. REGRESSION: 30/30 of existing test_attendance.py + test_reports.py still passing. Frontend Playwright: admin login (bsn.1988/12abAB!?) routes to /admin, sidebar shows 'النسخ الاحتياطي', /admin/backup renders BackupPage. create-backup-now produces backup-row-{id} with type chip + count chips. download-{id} triggers a real browser download (.json file). restore-{id} opens restore-dialog with name/date/size + amber 'before_restore_*' warning. restore-cancel closes dialog. restore-confirm POSTs and a backup-row with type chip 'قبل استرجاع' (before_restore) appears in list afterward. After deleting down to a single backup, attempting delete-{id} on the last one yields 400 from API (Arabic toast). When 2+ exist, delete succeeds. import-file-input accepts a JSON snapshot and produces an 'مستورد' (import) row. schedule-hour + save-schedule PATCH /admin/backups/settings with backup_hour HH:MM; persistence verified across cold reload (value=05:25 survived). TopBar SyncIndicator: sync-indicator-online dot visible online (green), click opens sync-popover with Arabic status. With navigator.onLine=false + window 'offline' event, sync-indicator-offline appears. Audit log table populates with op/backup_name/created_at/user_name/status rows. ALL test data cleaned at end via direct mongo: db.backups.deleteMany({})=>1 doc removed, db.backup_log.deleteMany({})=>16 entries removed, backup_hour reset to 02:00 default.
critical_code_review_comments: backup.py uses {'_id': 0, 'data': 0} projection on the list endpoint correctly preventing the 16MB embedded JSON payload from being shipped on every refresh — good.
_create_snapshot strips the auto-inserted _id from the doc before returning (line 130). FastAPI would otherwise fail to JSON-serialise an ObjectId. Defensive and correct.

Restore endpoint ALWAYS materialises a 'before_restore_*' safety snapshot BEFORE applying the dump. _apply_restore is per-collection (delete_many → insert_many), so a failure mid-restore can leave the DB in a half-restored state — but the safety backup is already on disk so the operator can re-restore from it. Acceptable for v1; consider transactions on a replica set later.

Retention prune only fires for type='auto' (line 135) — manual/import/before_restore snapshots accumulate forever. For v1 this matches the PRD ('up to 365 daily snapshots'), but if operators import frequently the UI list will grow unbounded. Worth a follow-up note.

_is_valid_snapshot is permissive — accepts any dict with .data:dict containing at least one list-typed COLLECTION key. Imported file with only {'data':{'students':[]}} passes. This is intentional per the prompt (auto-wraps raw data blobs), but means a partial dump can be imported and a later restore would WIPE the missing collections. Recommend the UI show a clear pre-restore counts diff so operators can spot it (CountChips already do this — good).

Scheduler is a single in-process asyncio task. On a multi-worker deploy (e.g. uvicorn --workers 4) every worker would create its own snapshot at the same minute → up to N duplicate auto_<date> rows per day until the same-day guard kicks in. The guard uses find_one({type:auto, created_at>=day_start}) — race-condition possible if all workers tick simultaneously. Mitigation: a unique index on (type, day) or run with --workers 1. Not blocking for current Render Free deploy (1 worker).
Frontend BackupPage.downloadOne uses fetch + Authorization header (instead of <a href>) so the bearer token never lands in the URL — good. URL.revokeObjectURL is called after click — no memory leak.

SyncIndicator priority (offline > syncing > online) matches spec; click toggles popover and outside-click handler closes it. data-testid dynamically reflects state (sync-indicator-{online|offline|syncing}) which is exactly what the test plan asks for.

Minor Playwright-only gotcha (NOT an app bug): page.fill on <input type='time'> in this React stack does not always dispatch React's synthetic onChange — saving via UI through Playwright requires manually dispatching an 'input' event (verified). Real users typing in the field work fine. Worth a note for future test scripts.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all spec items verified; pending-count badge path inferred from offline-queue infra carried over from iteration 4)

test_credentials: Admin: bsn.1988 / 12abAB!? (works). Teacher: nivin / 123456 (403 on /api/admin/backups* — correct).
seed_data_creation: During testing: 4 manual backups, 1 before_restore safety snapshot, 1 import row, 16 audit-log entries. ALL CLEANED at end via direct mongo: db.backups.deleteMany({}) and db.backup_log.deleteMany({}). backup_hour setting reset to 02:00 default. Verified DB state: backups=0, log=0.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backup endpoints live at /api/admin/backups (note: NO trailing slash on POST '' or GET ''). Pytest file /app/backend/tests/test_backup.py is a complete regression for this feature. For Playwright on type='time' inputs in this stack, use page.evaluate to set value via the native HTMLInputElement.prototype.value setter then dispatch 'input' event — page.fill/keyboard.type do not reliably trigger React's onChange. SyncIndicator test ids are dynamic: sync-indicator-online | sync-indicator-offline | sync-indicator-syncing — query with [data-testid^='sync-indicator-']. Restore dialog has data-testid='restore-dialog' on DialogContent (radix dialog renders into a portal — still queryable). Frontend delete uses window.confirm — register page.on('dialog', ...) to auto-accept in Playwright.
rca of the issue: No real bug. One investigation artefact: initial Playwright test reported schedule-hour as not persisting (saved 04:15, reloaded showed 02:00). RCA: this was a TEST-HARNESS artefact — Playwright page.fill / keyboard.type on a React-controlled <input type='time'> does not always dispatch React's synthetic onChange, so the React state remained '02:00' and the PATCH actually sent backup_hour=02:00. Direct curl PATCH→GET shows 04:15 round-trip correctly; subsequent Playwright tests that used the native value setter + manual 'input' event dispatch confirmed 05:25 survived a hard reload. Backend & frontend are correct.
